---
title: "EPL INDEPENDET MODEL"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-15"
---

```{r}
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# INDEPENDENT HOME WIN PREDICTION MODEL
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# KEY DIFFERENCE FROM GOALS PIPELINE:
#
#   Goals pipeline (what you did before):
#     xG + Elo → predict goals (lambda) → Poisson matrix → p_home
#     Two steps — information is lost in the matrix conversion
#
#   This model:
#     xG + Elo → predict home_win directly (binary logistic / XGBoost)
#     One step — no Poisson matrix, no bookmaker input
#
# DATA STRUCTURE DIFFERENCE:
#   Goals pipeline: two rows per match (one per team)
#   This model:     one row per match, home and away features side by side
#
# Dependent  : home_win (1 = home win, 0 = draw or away win)
# Independent: rolling xG features + elo_diff (NO p_home)
# Bookmaker  : used only for comparison and edge analysis, never as predictor
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════



library(tidyverse)
library(zoo)
library(xgboost)
library(purrr)
library(glmnet)
library(ranger)
library(car)

# Force dplyr functions to take priority over conflicting packages
select  <- dplyr::select
filter  <- dplyr::filter
recode  <- dplyr::recode
mutate  <- dplyr::mutate
rename  <- dplyr::rename


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 1: LOAD DATA
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

match_xg   <- readRDS("epl_match_xg.rds")
pl         <- readRDS("pl_clean.rds")
elo_by_date <- readRDS("elo_by_date.rds")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 2: PREPARE BOOKMAKER DATA
# Used for comparison only — NOT as a predictor
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

team_map <- c(
  "Man City"      = "Manchester City",
  "Man United"    = "Manchester United",
  "Newcastle"     = "Newcastle United",
  "Nott'm Forest" = "Nottingham Forest",
  "West Brom"     = "West Bromwich Albion",
  "Wolves"        = "Wolverhampton Wanderers"
)

pl <- pl %>%
  mutate(
    HomeTeam  = recode(HomeTeam, !!!team_map),
    AwayTeam  = recode(AwayTeam, !!!team_map),
    Date      = as.Date(Date),
    home_win  = as.integer(FTR == "H"),
    impl_h    = 1 / B365H,
    impl_d    = 1 / B365D,
    impl_a    = 1 / B365A,
    overround = impl_h + impl_d + impl_a,
    p_home    = impl_h / overround
  ) %>%
  select(Date, HomeTeam, AwayTeam, home_win, p_home)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 3: RESHAPE MATCH XG TO TEAM LEVEL
# Needed to compute per-team rolling averages correctly
# before collapsing back to match level
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

model_df <- bind_rows(
  match_xg %>% transmute(
    match_id, season,
    date        = as.Date(date),
    team        = home_team,
    opponent    = away_team,
    home_away   = "home",
    xG_openplay = home_xG_openplay,
    xG_corner   = home_xG_corner,
    xG_freekick = home_xG_freekick,
    xG_penalty  = home_xG_penalty,
    xG_setpiece = home_xG_setpiece
  ),
  match_xg %>% transmute(
    match_id, season,
    date        = as.Date(date),
    team        = away_team,
    opponent    = home_team,
    home_away   = "away",
    xG_openplay = away_xG_openplay,
    xG_corner   = away_xG_corner,
    xG_freekick = away_xG_freekick,
    xG_penalty  = away_xG_penalty,
    xG_setpiece = away_xG_setpiece
  )
) %>%
  arrange(team, date)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 4: EXPONENTIALLY WEIGHTED ROLLING AVERAGES
# Same weighting scheme as goals pipeline for consistency
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

exp_weights <- exp(seq(0, 1, length.out = 5))
exp_weights <- exp_weights / sum(exp_weights)
wmean5      <- function(x) weighted.mean(x, w = exp_weights)

model_df <- model_df %>%
  group_by(team) %>%
  mutate(
    roll_xG_openplay = lag(rollapply(xG_openplay, 5, FUN = wmean5,
                                     fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_corner   = lag(rollapply(xG_corner,   5, FUN = wmean5,
                                     fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_freekick = lag(rollapply(xG_freekick, 5, FUN = wmean5,
                                     fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_penalty  = lag(rollapply(xG_penalty,  5, FUN = wmean5,
                                     fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_setpiece = lag(rollapply(xG_setpiece, 5, FUN = wmean5,
                                     fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    sd_xG            = lag(rollapply(
      xG_openplay + xG_corner + xG_freekick + xG_penalty + xG_setpiece,
      5, FUN = sd, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 5: COLLAPSE TO ONE ROW PER MATCH
# This is the key structural difference from the goals pipeline
# Goals pipeline kept two rows per match
# This model collapses to one row with home and away features
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

home_feats <- model_df %>%
  filter(home_away == "home") %>%
  select(
    match_id, season, date,
    home_team             = team,
    away_team             = opponent,
    home_roll_xG_openplay = roll_xG_openplay,
    home_roll_xG_corner   = roll_xG_corner,
    home_roll_xG_freekick = roll_xG_freekick,
    home_roll_xG_penalty  = roll_xG_penalty,
    home_roll_xG_setpiece = roll_xG_setpiece,
    home_sd_xG            = sd_xG
  )

away_feats <- model_df %>%
  filter(home_away == "away") %>%
  select(
    match_id,
    away_roll_xG_openplay = roll_xG_openplay,
    away_roll_xG_corner   = roll_xG_corner,
    away_roll_xG_freekick = roll_xG_freekick,
    away_roll_xG_penalty  = roll_xG_penalty,
    away_roll_xG_setpiece = roll_xG_setpiece,
    away_sd_xG            = sd_xG
  )

match_df <- match_xg %>%
  transmute(
    match_id,
    date      = as.Date(date),
    season,
    home_team,
    away_team,
    home_win  = as.integer(home_goals > away_goals)
  ) %>%
  left_join(home_feats, by = c("match_id", "season", "date",
                                "home_team", "away_team")) %>%
  left_join(away_feats, by = "match_id") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  arrange(date)

cat("Match-level rows:", nrow(match_df), "\n")
cat("Home win rate:   ", round(mean(match_df$home_win), 3), "\n")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 6: JOIN ELO RATINGS
# elo_diff = home team Elo minus away team Elo
# Captures structural quality difference independent of recent form
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

elo_name_map <- c(
  "Man City"   = "Manchester City",
  "Man United" = "Manchester United",
  "West Brom"  = "West Bromwich Albion",
  "Newcastle"  = "Newcastle United",
  "Wolves"     = "Wolverhampton Wanderers",
  "Forest"     = "Nottingham Forest"
)

elo_lookup <- elo_by_date %>%
  mutate(
    Club = recode(Club, !!!elo_name_map),
    date = as.Date(date),
    Elo  = as.numeric(Elo)
  ) %>%
  select(date, Club, Elo)

match_df <- match_df %>%
  left_join(elo_lookup %>% rename(home_elo = Elo),
            by = c("date", "home_team" = "Club")) %>%
  left_join(elo_lookup %>% rename(away_elo = Elo),
            by = c("date", "away_team" = "Club")) %>%
  mutate(elo_diff = home_elo - away_elo) %>%
  drop_na(elo_diff)

cat("Missing Elo:", sum(is.na(match_df$elo_diff)), "\n")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 7: JOIN BOOKMAKER PROBABILITY (for comparison only)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

match_df <- match_df %>%
  left_join(
    pl,
    by = c("date"      = "Date",
           "home_team" = "HomeTeam",
           "away_team" = "AwayTeam")
  ) %>%
  # home_win already in match_df from match_xg — drop pl duplicate
  select(-home_win.y) %>%
  rename(home_win = home_win.x)

cat("Rows with p_home: ", sum(!is.na(match_df$p_home)), "\n")
cat("Rows missing:     ", sum(is.na(match_df$p_home)),  "\n")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 8: TRAIN / TEST SPLIT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

match_df <- match_df %>%
  drop_na(p_home,
          home_roll_xG_openplay, home_sd_xG,
          away_roll_xG_openplay, away_sd_xG,
          elo_diff)

train_df <- match_df %>% filter(season <= 2022)
test_df  <- match_df %>% filter(season >= 2023)

cat("Train rows:", nrow(train_df), "| Seasons:",
    min(train_df$season), "-", max(train_df$season), "\n")
cat("Test rows: ", nrow(test_df),  "| Seasons:",
    min(test_df$season),  "-", max(test_df$season),  "\n")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 9: EVALUATION FUNCTIONS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

log_loss <- function(actual, predicted) {
  p <- pmax(pmin(predicted, 1 - 1e-15), 1e-15)
  -mean(actual * log(p) + (1 - actual) * log(1 - p))
}

brier_score <- function(actual, predicted) {
  mean((actual - predicted)^2)
}

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 10: LOGISTIC REGRESSION — INDEPENDENT MODEL
# No p_home anywhere — purely xG and Elo features
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Model 1 — Elo only (baseline for independent model)
logit_elo <- glm(
  home_win ~ elo_diff,
  data   = train_df,
  family = binomial(link = "logit")
)
summary(logit_elo)

# Model 2 — Elo + open play xG only
logit_elo_xg <- glm(
  home_win ~
    elo_diff +
    home_roll_xG_openplay +
    away_roll_xG_openplay,
  data   = train_df,
  family = binomial(link = "logit")
)
summary(logit_elo_xg)

# Model 3 — Elo + all xG situation types + SD
logit_full <- glm(
  home_win ~
    elo_diff +
    home_roll_xG_openplay +
    home_roll_xG_corner   +
    home_roll_xG_freekick +
    home_roll_xG_penalty  +
    home_roll_xG_setpiece +
    home_sd_xG            +
    away_roll_xG_openplay +
    away_roll_xG_corner   +
    away_roll_xG_freekick +
    away_roll_xG_penalty  +
    away_roll_xG_setpiece +
    away_sd_xG,
  data   = train_df,
  family = binomial(link = "logit")
)
summary(logit_full)

cat("\n=== AIC Comparison (logistic) ===\n")
cat("Elo only:        ", AIC(logit_elo),    "\n")
cat("Elo + open play: ", AIC(logit_elo_xg), "\n")
cat("Elo + all xG:    ", AIC(logit_full),   "\n")

test_df <- test_df %>%
  mutate(
    pred_logit_elo    = predict(logit_elo,    newdata = test_df, type = "response"),
    pred_logit_elo_xg = predict(logit_elo_xg, newdata = test_df, type = "response"),
    pred_logit_full   = predict(logit_full,   newdata = test_df, type = "response")
  )

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 11: LASSO LOGISTIC REGRESSION
# Automatic feature selection — lets the data decide which
# xG situation types add value beyond Elo
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

feature_names <- c(
  "elo_diff",
  "home_roll_xG_openplay", "home_roll_xG_corner",
  "home_roll_xG_freekick", "home_roll_xG_penalty",
  "home_roll_xG_setpiece", "home_sd_xG",
  "away_roll_xG_openplay", "away_roll_xG_corner",
  "away_roll_xG_freekick", "away_roll_xG_penalty",
  "away_roll_xG_setpiece", "away_sd_xG"
)

x_train <- as.matrix(train_df[, feature_names])
y_train <- train_df$home_win
x_test  <- as.matrix(test_df[, feature_names])

# Cross-validated Lasso — finds optimal lambda
set.seed(42)
lasso_cv <- cv.glmnet(
  x_train, y_train,
  family  = "binomial",
  alpha   = 1,           # 1 = Lasso, 0 = Ridge, between = Elastic Net
  nfolds  = 10
)

cat("\n=== Lasso optimal lambda ===\n")
cat("Lambda min:    ", round(lasso_cv$lambda.min,    6), "\n")
cat("Lambda 1se:    ", round(lasso_cv$lambda.1se,    6), "\n")

# Coefficients at optimal lambda — shows which features survived
cat("\n=== Lasso selected features (lambda.1se) ===\n")
print(coef(lasso_cv, s = "lambda.1se"))

test_df <- test_df %>%
  mutate(
    pred_lasso = as.vector(
      predict(lasso_cv, newx = x_test,
              s = "lambda.1se", type = "response")
    )
  )

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 12: XGBOOST — BINARY CLASSIFICATION
# Objective = binary:logistic — outputs probability directly
# No Poisson matrix conversion needed
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

train_matrix <- xgb.DMatrix(
  data  = x_train,
  label = y_train
)

test_matrix <- xgb.DMatrix(
  data  = x_test,
  label = test_df$home_win
)

xgb_model <- xgb.train(
  params = list(
    objective        = "binary:logistic",
    eval_metric      = "logloss",
    eta              = 0.05,
    max_depth        = 4,
    subsample        = 0.8,
    colsample_bytree = 0.8
  ),
  data                  = train_matrix,
  nrounds               = 500,
  evals                 = list(train = train_matrix, test = test_matrix),
  early_stopping_rounds = 30,
  verbose               = 1,
  print_every_n         = 50
)

test_df <- test_df %>%
  mutate(pred_xgb = predict(xgb_model, test_matrix))

# Feature importance
importance <- xgb.importance(feature_names = feature_names, model = xgb_model)
cat("\n=== XGBoost Feature Importance ===\n")
print(importance)
xgb.plot.importance(importance)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 13: RANDOM FOREST
# Tree ensemble — captures non-linear interactions
# probability = TRUE outputs calibrated probabilities
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

rf_train <- train_df %>%
  select(all_of(feature_names), home_win) %>%
  mutate(home_win = as.factor(home_win))

rf_test <- test_df %>%
  select(all_of(feature_names))

rf_model <- ranger(
  home_win ~ .,
  data        = rf_train,
  num.trees   = 500,
  probability = TRUE,
  importance  = "impurity",
  seed        = 42
)

# Extract probability of home win (class "1")
test_df <- test_df %>%
  mutate(pred_rf = predict(rf_model, data = rf_test)$predictions[, "1"])

cat("\n=== Random Forest Variable Importance ===\n")
print(sort(rf_model$variable.importance, decreasing = TRUE))

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 14: FORECAST COMBINATIONS
# Average predictions across models
# Different models make errors in different situations —
# combining them reduces variance and often beats all individuals
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

test_df <- test_df %>%
  mutate(
    # Equal weighted combination of all four models
    pred_combo_equal = (pred_logit_full + pred_lasso +
                          pred_xgb + pred_rf) / 4,

    # Combination without Lasso (logit, XGBoost, RF)
    pred_combo_3 = (pred_logit_full + pred_xgb + pred_rf) / 3
  )

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 15: EVALUATE ALL MODELS vs BOOKMAKER
# This is the key comparison — our independent probabilities
# against the bookmaker's fair probability
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

eval_results <- test_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(p_home)) %>%
  summarise(
    # Bookmaker baseline
    ll_bookmaker       = log_loss(home_win, p_home),
    bs_bookmaker       = brier_score(home_win, p_home),
    # Independent models
    ll_logit_elo       = log_loss(home_win, pred_logit_elo),
    ll_logit_elo_xg    = log_loss(home_win, pred_logit_elo_xg),
    ll_logit_full      = log_loss(home_win, pred_logit_full),
    ll_lasso           = log_loss(home_win, pred_lasso),
    ll_xgb             = log_loss(home_win, pred_xgb),
    ll_rf              = log_loss(home_win, pred_rf),
    ll_combo_equal     = log_loss(home_win, pred_combo_equal),
    ll_combo_3         = log_loss(home_win, pred_combo_3),
    bs_logit_elo       = brier_score(home_win, pred_logit_elo),
    bs_logit_elo_xg    = brier_score(home_win, pred_logit_elo_xg),
    bs_logit_full      = brier_score(home_win, pred_logit_full),
    bs_lasso           = brier_score(home_win, pred_lasso),
    bs_xgb             = brier_score(home_win, pred_xgb),
    bs_rf              = brier_score(home_win, pred_rf),
    bs_combo_equal     = brier_score(home_win, pred_combo_equal),
    bs_combo_3         = brier_score(home_win, pred_combo_3)
  )

cat("\n=== Model Evaluation vs Bookmaker (test set) ===\n")
cat("                      Log Loss   Brier\n")
cat("Bookmaker (p_home):   ",
    round(eval_results$ll_bookmaker,    4), " ",
    round(eval_results$bs_bookmaker,    4), "\n")
cat("Logit Elo only:       ",
    round(eval_results$ll_logit_elo,    4), " ",
    round(eval_results$bs_logit_elo,    4), "\n")
cat("Logit Elo + open play:",
    round(eval_results$ll_logit_elo_xg, 4), " ",
    round(eval_results$bs_logit_elo_xg, 4), "\n")
cat("Logit full:           ",
    round(eval_results$ll_logit_full,   4), " ",
    round(eval_results$bs_logit_full,   4), "\n")
cat("Lasso:                ",
    round(eval_results$ll_lasso,        4), " ",
    round(eval_results$bs_lasso,        4), "\n")
cat("XGBoost:              ",
    round(eval_results$ll_xgb,          4), " ",
    round(eval_results$bs_xgb,          4), "\n")
cat("Random Forest:        ",
    round(eval_results$ll_rf,           4), " ",
    round(eval_results$bs_rf,           4), "\n")
cat("Combo (4 models):     ",
    round(eval_results$ll_combo_equal,  4), " ",
    round(eval_results$bs_combo_equal,  4), "\n")
cat("Combo (3 models):     ",
    round(eval_results$ll_combo_3,      4), " ",
    round(eval_results$bs_combo_3,      4), "\n")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ROBUSTNESS CHECK — SIMPLE MEAN vs EXPONENTIAL WEIGHTED MEAN
# The current model uses exponential weighting (wmean5)
# Here we refit using simple rollmean to compare directly
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

model_df_simple <- bind_rows(
  match_xg %>% transmute(
    match_id, season, date = as.Date(date),
    team = home_team, opponent = away_team, home_away = "home",
    xG_openplay = home_xG_openplay, xG_corner = home_xG_corner,
    xG_freekick = home_xG_freekick, xG_penalty = home_xG_penalty,
    xG_setpiece = home_xG_setpiece
  ),
  match_xg %>% transmute(
    match_id, season, date = as.Date(date),
    team = away_team, opponent = home_team, home_away = "away",
    xG_openplay = away_xG_openplay, xG_corner = away_xG_corner,
    xG_freekick = away_xG_freekick, xG_penalty = away_xG_penalty,
    xG_setpiece = away_xG_setpiece
  )
) %>%
  arrange(team, date) %>%
  group_by(team) %>%
  mutate(
    roll_xG_openplay = lag(rollmean(xG_openplay, 5, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_corner   = lag(rollmean(xG_corner,   5, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_freekick = lag(rollmean(xG_freekick, 5, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_penalty  = lag(rollmean(xG_penalty,  5, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_setpiece = lag(rollmean(xG_setpiece, 5, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    sd_xG            = lag(rollapply(
      xG_openplay + xG_corner + xG_freekick + xG_penalty + xG_setpiece,
      5, FUN = sd, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

home_simple <- model_df_simple %>%
  filter(home_away == "home") %>%
  select(match_id, season, date, home_team = team, away_team = opponent,
         home_roll_xG_openplay = roll_xG_openplay,
         home_roll_xG_corner   = roll_xG_corner,
         home_roll_xG_freekick = roll_xG_freekick,
         home_roll_xG_penalty  = roll_xG_penalty,
         home_roll_xG_setpiece = roll_xG_setpiece,
         home_sd_xG            = sd_xG)

away_simple <- model_df_simple %>%
  filter(home_away == "away") %>%
  select(match_id,
         away_roll_xG_openplay = roll_xG_openplay,
         away_roll_xG_corner   = roll_xG_corner,
         away_roll_xG_freekick = roll_xG_freekick,
         away_roll_xG_penalty  = roll_xG_penalty,
         away_roll_xG_setpiece = roll_xG_setpiece,
         away_sd_xG            = sd_xG)

match_df_simple <- match_xg %>%
  transmute(match_id, date = as.Date(date), season,
            home_team, away_team,
            home_win = as.integer(home_goals > away_goals)) %>%
  left_join(home_simple, by = c("match_id", "season", "date",
                                 "home_team", "away_team")) %>%
  left_join(away_simple, by = "match_id") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  arrange(date) %>%
  left_join(elo_lookup %>% rename(home_elo = Elo),
            by = c("date", "home_team" = "Club")) %>%
  left_join(elo_lookup %>% rename(away_elo = Elo),
            by = c("date", "away_team" = "Club")) %>%
  mutate(elo_diff = home_elo - away_elo) %>%
  drop_na(elo_diff) %>%
  left_join(pl, by = c("date" = "Date",
                        "home_team" = "HomeTeam",
                        "away_team" = "AwayTeam")) %>%
  select(-home_win.y) %>%
  rename(home_win = home_win.x) %>%
  drop_na(p_home)

train_simple <- match_df_simple %>% filter(season <= 2022)
test_simple  <- match_df_simple %>% filter(season >= 2023)

# Fit same logit full model on simple mean features
logit_simple <- glm(
  home_win ~
    elo_diff +
    home_roll_xG_openplay + home_roll_xG_corner +
    home_roll_xG_freekick + home_roll_xG_penalty +
    home_roll_xG_setpiece + home_sd_xG +
    away_roll_xG_openplay + away_roll_xG_corner +
    away_roll_xG_freekick + away_roll_xG_penalty +
    away_roll_xG_setpiece + away_sd_xG,
  data   = train_simple,
  family = binomial(link = "logit")
)

test_simple <- test_simple %>%
  mutate(pred_simple = predict(logit_simple,
                               newdata = test_simple,
                               type    = "response"))

cat("\n=== Simple mean vs Exponential weighted mean ===\n")
cat("                         Log Loss   Brier\n")
cat("Bookmaker:               ",
    round(log_loss(test_simple$home_win, test_simple$p_home),   4), " ",
    round(brier_score(test_simple$home_win, test_simple$p_home), 4), "\n")
cat("Logit simple mean:       ",
    round(log_loss(test_simple$home_win, test_simple$pred_simple),   4), " ",
    round(brier_score(test_simple$home_win, test_simple$pred_simple), 4), "\n")
cat("Logit exp weighted mean: ",
    round(log_loss(test_df$home_win, test_df$pred_logit_full),   4), " ",
    round(brier_score(test_df$home_win, test_df$pred_logit_full), 4), "\n")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 16: CALIBRATION CHECK
# Does our predicted probability match actual win rates?
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

calibration_df <- test_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(p_home)) %>%
  mutate(
    prob_bucket = cut(pred_logit_full,
                      breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1),
                      include.lowest = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  group_by(prob_bucket) %>%
  summarise(
    n               = n(),
    avg_predicted   = round(mean(pred_logit_full), 4),
    actual_win_rate = round(mean(home_win),         4),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n=== Calibration (logit full model) ===\n")
print(calibration_df)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 17: EDGE ANALYSIS
# edge = our probability - bookmaker fair probability
# Positive = we think home team is more likely to win than market
# Negative = we think home team is less likely to win than market
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

eval_df <- test_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(p_home)) %>%
  mutate(
    edge_logit = pred_logit_full  - p_home,
    edge_xgb   = pred_xgb         - p_home,
    edge_rf    = pred_rf           - p_home,
    edge_combo = pred_combo_equal  - p_home
  )

edge_analysis <- eval_df %>%
  mutate(
    edge_bucket = cut(edge_logit,
                      breaks = c(-1, -0.2, -0.1, 0, 0.1, 0.2, 1),
                      labels = c("Strong against", "Mild against",
                                 "Neutral low",    "Neutral high",
                                 "Mild for",       "Strong for"))
  ) %>%
  group_by(edge_bucket) %>%
  summarise(
    n              = n(),
    avg_edge       = round(mean(edge_logit),     4),
    actual_rate    = round(mean(home_win),        4),
    avg_model_prob = round(mean(pred_logit_full), 4),
    avg_fair_prob  = round(mean(p_home),          4),
    log_loss_model = round(log_loss(home_win, pred_logit_full), 4),
    log_loss_book  = round(log_loss(home_win, p_home),          4),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n=== Edge analysis (logit full) ===\n")
print(edge_analysis)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 18: SIMULATED BETTING ROI
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

betting_returns <- function(df, edge_threshold, pred_col = "pred_logit_full") {
  bets <- df %>%
    mutate(edge = .data[[pred_col]] - p_home) %>%
    filter(edge > edge_threshold) %>%
    mutate(
      decimal_odds = 1 / p_home,
      profit       = if_else(home_win == 1, decimal_odds - 1, -1)
    )

  if (nrow(bets) == 0) {
    return(tibble(threshold = edge_threshold, n_bets = 0,
                  total_profit = NA, roi = NA,
                  win_rate = NA, avg_edge = NA))
  }

  tibble(
    threshold    = edge_threshold,
    n_bets       = nrow(bets),
    total_profit = round(sum(bets$profit),         2),
    roi          = round(sum(bets$profit) /
                           nrow(bets) * 100,        2),
    win_rate     = round(mean(bets$home_win) * 100, 2),
    avg_edge     = round(mean(bets$edge),           3)
  )
}

thresholds <- c(0.05, 0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.25, 0.30)

roi_logit <- map_dfr(thresholds, ~betting_returns(eval_df, .x, "pred_logit_full"))
roi_xgb   <- map_dfr(thresholds, ~betting_returns(eval_df, .x, "pred_xgb"))
roi_rf    <- map_dfr(thresholds, ~betting_returns(eval_df, .x, "pred_rf"))
roi_combo <- map_dfr(thresholds, ~betting_returns(eval_df, .x, "pred_combo_equal"))

cat("\n=== Simulated ROI — Logit full ===\n");  print(roi_logit)
cat("\n=== Simulated ROI — XGBoost ===\n");     print(roi_xgb)
cat("\n=== Simulated ROI — Random Forest ===\n"); print(roi_rf)
cat("\n=== Simulated ROI — Combination ===\n"); print(roi_combo)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 19: STATISTICAL TEST
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

model_losses <- eval_df %>%
  mutate(
    loss_model = -(home_win * log(pmax(pred_logit_full, 1e-15)) +
                   (1 - home_win) * log(pmax(1 - pred_logit_full, 1e-15))),
    loss_book  = -(home_win * log(pmax(p_home, 1e-15)) +
                   (1 - home_win) * log(pmax(1 - p_home, 1e-15))),
    diff       = loss_model - loss_book
  )

t_result <- t.test(model_losses$diff, mu = 0)

cat("\n=== Statistical test: independent model vs bookmaker ===\n")
cat("Mean difference (model - bookmaker):",
    round(mean(model_losses$diff), 4), "\n")
cat("Negative = model is better\n")
cat("p-value:", round(t_result$p.value, 4), "\n")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SAVE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

saveRDS(match_df, "epl_independent_match_df.rds")
saveRDS(test_df,  "epl_independent_test_results.rds")

cat("\nDone.\n")
```



TESTING FOR LONGSHOTS BIAS

```{r}
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FAVOURITE-LONGSHOT BIAS RECALIBRATION
#
# The bookmaker systematically overestimates longshots
# and underestimates strong favourites. This script fits
# a correction model and tests whether it improves predictions.
#
# Requires: train_df and test_df from independent model script
#           with columns: home_win, p_home
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

library(tidyverse)
library(purrr)

select <- dplyr::select
filter <- dplyr::filter
mutate <- dplyr::mutate

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# EVALUATION FUNCTIONS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

log_loss <- function(actual, predicted) {
  p <- pmax(pmin(predicted, 1 - 1e-15), 1e-15)
  -mean(actual * log(p) + (1 - actual) * log(1 - p))
}

brier_score <- function(actual, predicted) {
  mean((actual - predicted)^2)
}

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 1: VISUALISE THE BIAS IN TRAINING DATA
# Before fitting any correction, confirm the bias exists
# and understand its shape
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

bias_by_bucket <- train_df %>%
  mutate(
    p_bucket = cut(p_home,
                   breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1),
                   include.lowest = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  group_by(p_bucket) %>%
  summarise(
    n           = n(),
    avg_implied = round(mean(p_home),   4),
    actual_rate = round(mean(home_win), 4),
    bias        = round(mean(home_win) - mean(p_home), 4),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("=== Bias by probability bucket (training set) ===\n")
cat("Negative bias = bookmaker overestimates (longshots)\n")
cat("Positive bias = bookmaker underestimates (favourites)\n")
print(bias_by_bucket)

ggplot(bias_by_bucket, aes(x = avg_implied, y = bias)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = bias > 0), show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "steelblue", "FALSE" = "tomato")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Favourite-Longshot Bias",
    subtitle = "Red = bookmaker overestimates (longshots)  |  Blue = bookmaker underestimates (favourites)",
    x        = "Bookmaker implied probability",
    y        = "Actual rate minus implied probability"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 2: FIT RECALIBRATION MODELS
#
# Model A — Linear recalibration
#   Simply rescales the bookmaker probability
#   If intercept ~0 and slope ~1 the bookmaker is well calibrated
#
# Model B — Quadratic recalibration
#   Adds p_home^2 to capture the non-linear bias pattern
#   The quadratic term should be negative if favourite-longshot
#   bias exists — bookmaker overestimates at both extremes
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Model A — Linear
recal_linear <- glm(
  home_win ~ p_home,
  data   = train_df,
  family = binomial(link = "logit")
)

summary(recal_linear)
cat("\n=== Linear recalibration ===\n")
cat("A well calibrated bookmaker has intercept ~0 and slope ~1\n")
cat("on the log-odds scale (logit(p_home) ~= 1)\n")

# Model B — Quadratic
recal_quad <- glm(
  home_win ~ p_home + I(p_home^2),
  data   = train_df,
  family = binomial(link = "logit")
)

summary(recal_quad)
cat("\n=== Quadratic recalibration ===\n")
cat("If p_home^2 coefficient is significant:\n")
cat("  Negative = U-shaped bias (over at extremes, under in middle)\n")
cat("  Positive = Inverse U-shaped bias\n")

# AIC comparison between the two recalibration models
cat("\n=== AIC: linear vs quadratic recalibration ===\n")
cat("Linear:    ", AIC(recal_linear), "\n")
cat("Quadratic: ", AIC(recal_quad),   "\n")
cat("Lower AIC = better fit — if quadratic wins, the non-linear bias is real\n")

# Generate predictions
test_df <- test_df %>%
  mutate(
    pred_recal_linear = predict(recal_linear, newdata = test_df, type = "response"),
    pred_recal_quad   = predict(recal_quad,   newdata = test_df, type = "response")
  )

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 3: EVALUATE — DOES RECALIBRATION IMPROVE PREDICTIONS?
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

eval_df <- test_df %>% filter(!is.na(p_home))

results <- tibble(
  model = c(
    "Bookmaker raw",
    "Recalibrated linear",
    "Recalibrated quadratic"
  ),
  log_loss = c(
    log_loss(eval_df$home_win, eval_df$p_home),
    log_loss(eval_df$home_win, eval_df$pred_recal_linear),
    log_loss(eval_df$home_win, eval_df$pred_recal_quad)
  ),
  brier = c(
    brier_score(eval_df$home_win, eval_df$p_home),
    brier_score(eval_df$home_win, eval_df$pred_recal_linear),
    brier_score(eval_df$home_win, eval_df$pred_recal_quad)
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    log_loss = round(log_loss, 4),
    brier    = round(brier,    4)
  )

cat("\n=== Model evaluation (test set) ===\n")
cat("If recalibrated log loss < bookmaker log loss: correction helps\n")
print(results)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 4: CALIBRATION CHECK
# Does recalibration actually reduce the bias bucket by bucket?
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cal_check <- eval_df %>%
  mutate(
    p_bucket = cut(p_home, breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1), include.lowest = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  group_by(p_bucket) %>%
  summarise(
    n            = n(),
    actual_rate  = round(mean(home_win),           4),
    raw_pred     = round(mean(p_home),              4),
    recal_pred   = round(mean(pred_recal_quad),     4),
    bias_raw     = round(mean(home_win) - mean(p_home),         4),
    bias_recal   = round(mean(home_win) - mean(pred_recal_quad), 4),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n=== Calibration comparison: raw vs recalibrated ===\n")
cat("bias_recal closer to zero = recalibration worked in that bucket\n")
print(cal_check)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 5: STATISTICAL TEST
# Is the improvement statistically significant?
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

loss_raw   <- -(eval_df$home_win * log(pmax(eval_df$p_home, 1e-15)) +
                (1 - eval_df$home_win) * log(pmax(1 - eval_df$p_home, 1e-15)))

loss_recal <- -(eval_df$home_win * log(pmax(eval_df$pred_recal_quad, 1e-15)) +
                (1 - eval_df$home_win) * log(pmax(1 - eval_df$pred_recal_quad, 1e-15)))

t_result <- t.test(loss_recal - loss_raw, mu = 0)

cat("\n=== Statistical test: recalibrated vs raw bookmaker ===\n")
cat("Mean difference (recalibrated - raw):", round(mean(loss_recal - loss_raw), 4), "\n")
cat("Negative = recalibration improves on raw bookmaker\n")
cat("p-value:", round(t_result$p.value, 4), "\n")
cat("If p < 0.05: improvement is statistically significant\n")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 6: SIMULATED BETTING ROI
# Bet when recalibrated probability exceeds raw bookmaker probability
# i.e. when recalibration says the bookmaker is underestimating
# the home team — strong favourite situations
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

betting_returns <- function(df, threshold) {
  bets <- df %>%
    mutate(edge = pred_recal_quad - p_home) %>%
    filter(edge > threshold) %>%
    mutate(
      decimal_odds = 1 / p_home,
      profit       = if_else(home_win == 1, decimal_odds - 1, -1)
    )

  if (nrow(bets) == 0) {
    return(tibble(threshold = threshold, n_bets = 0,
                  total_profit = NA, roi = NA,
                  win_rate = NA, avg_edge = NA))
  }

  tibble(
    threshold    = threshold,
    n_bets       = nrow(bets),
    total_profit = round(sum(bets$profit),         2),
    roi          = round(sum(bets$profit) /
                           nrow(bets) * 100,        2),
    win_rate     = round(mean(bets$home_win) * 100, 2),
    avg_edge     = round(mean(bets$edge),           3)
  )
}

roi_table <- map_dfr(c(0.02, 0.03, 0.05, 0.10), ~betting_returns(eval_df, .x))

cat("\n=== Simulated ROI — betting on recalibration edge ===\n")
cat("Positive ROI = recalibration found exploitable edge\n")
print(roi_table)
```
